gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 high-reasoning review found a P1 double-writer bug in Databricks fixture target parameters, then a narrower P2 fixture-owned alias case. Fixed by suppressing fixture-owned actions in Databricks runner parameters and using canonical artifact-path equivalence; final delta review found no blocking issues.
verification: PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_benchmark_plan.py::test_build_v1_benchmark_plan_derives_fixture_actions_output_json tests/test_benchmark_plan.py::test_main_derives_fixture_actions_for_release_planned_engine_probes tests/test_benchmark_plan.py::test_main_can_derive_planned_engine_probe_handoff_from_fixture_output_dir tests/test_benchmark_plan.py::test_engine_probe_targets_record_can_feed_databricks_matrix_helper -q; PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_databricks_engine_probe_job.py::test_databricks_engine_probe_matrix_payload_skips_fixture_owned_actions_output tests/test_databricks_engine_probe_job.py::test_databricks_engine_probe_matrix_payload_skips_fixture_owned_actions_alias -q; PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_databricks_engine_probe_job.py tests/test_benchmark_plan.py -q (175 passed); PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q (1379 passed); python -m compileall -q src tests; git diff --check
what_changed: Fixture-backed planned engine probes now derive the connector-action sidecar path from qwen3-v1-fixture.actions.json when actions_output_json is omitted; direct benchmark-plan commands and Databricks matrix runner parameters avoid rewriting fixture-owned action sidecars while preserving explicit custom action outputs.
why: Release-safe fixture probe plans should not require a redundant actions-output argument, and fixture-generated connector actions should have one writer across local and Databricks execution paths.
